title: Component library file I/O utilities for saving, loading, and listing design components
purpose: Provides functions to persist and retrieve design component subtrees to/from the file system, enabling users to save and reuse component configurations across sessions.
module_context: Exports three main functions for managing component files: saving components to disk, loading them back, and listing all available components in the library.

steps.content: Construct and return the file system path to the components directory for a given project and session.
Replace any characters in the filename that are not alphanumeric, hyphens, or underscores with underscores.
Convert the sanitized filename to lowercase.

methods.name: saveComponentToLibrary
methods.normalized_params: (project: string, session: string, name: string, nodes: any[]): Promise<{ success: boolean; path: string }>

steps.content: Determine the components directory for the given project and session.
Create the components directory if it does not already exist.
Generate a sanitized filename for the component based on its name.
Construct the full file path for the component.
Prepare the component data including name, timestamp, and nodes.
Write the component data to the file in JSON format with indentation.
Return a success status and the path to the saved file.

methods.name: loadComponentFromLibrary
methods.normalized_params: (project: string, session: string, name: string): Promise<{ name: string; nodes: any[] }>

steps.content: Determine the components directory for the given project and session.
Generate a sanitized filename for the component based on its name.
Construct the full file path for the component.
Attempt to read the component file from disk.
IF the file does not exist, throw an error indicating the component is not found.
IF the file exists but cannot be read, re-throw the error.
Parse the file contents as JSON.
IF the parsed data is invalid, throw an error indicating corrupted data.
Validate that the parsed data contains a valid nodes array.
IF the nodes array is missing or invalid, throw an error indicating invalid format.
Return the component name and its nodes.

methods.name: listLibraryComponents
methods.normalized_params: (project: string, session: string): Promise<Array<{ name: string; filename: string; savedAt: string }>>

steps.content: Determine the components directory for the given project and session.
Attempt to read the list of files in the components directory.
IF the directory does not exist, return an empty list of components.
For each file in the directory, check if it has the component file extension.
IF the file is not a component file, skip to the next file.
Attempt to read and parse the component file.
IF the file cannot be parsed, skip to the next file.
Extract the component name, filename, and save timestamp from the parsed data.
Add the component information to the list of components.
Return the list of all valid components in the library.
